jobs.prompt: 16:9 educational editorial illustration for a lecture on asymptotic notation and dominant terms. Show several smooth growth-like curves or ribbons moving to the right, with one visually emerging as dominant while smaller components fade into the background, suggesting that asymptotics keeps the leading behavior and suppresses lower-order detail. The composition should feel mathematically disciplined, not decorative chaos. Warm paper background, deep ink structure, dusty blue, terracotta and sage accents, generous whitespace, premium academic style. No readable text, no digits, no formulas, no fake axes or dashboard graphics.

Educational editorial illustration for local asymptotic equivalence near a point. Show two almost indistinguishable smooth traces inside a magnified local neighborhood, suggesting that different formulas can share the same leading term near zero. The scene should communicate 'under the microscope they coincide locally' without any literal equations. Minimal, calm, academic, warm paper palette, no readable text, no clutter, no fake charts.
